# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
class Admin::ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin!
  before_action :authorize_reporting!

  # Predefined report templates that power users can select and parameterize
  ALLOWED_REPORTS = {
    "user_signups" => {
      sql: "SELECT DATE(created_at) AS signup_date, COUNT(*) AS total FROM users WHERE created_at BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date GROUP BY DATE(created_at) ORDER BY signup_date",
      params: [:start_date, :end_date],
      description: "Daily user signups within a date range"
    },
    "revenue_by_product" => {
      sql: "SELECT products.name, SUM(order_items.quantity * order_items.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items INNER JOIN products ON products.id = order_items.product_id INNER JOIN orders ON orders.id = order_items.order_id WHERE orders.created_at BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date GROUP BY products.name ORDER BY revenue DESC",
      params: [:start_date, :end_date],
      description: "Revenue breakdown by product"
    },
    "active_users" => {
      sql: "SELECT users.email, users.created_at, COUNT(sessions.id) AS session_count, MAX(sessions.created_at) AS last_active FROM users LEFT JOIN sessions ON sessions.user_id = users.id WHERE sessions.created_at >= :since_date GROUP BY users.id, users.email, users.created_at HAVING COUNT(sessions.id) >= :min_sessions ORDER BY session_count DESC LIMIT :row_limit",
      params: [:since_date, :min_sessions, :row_limit],
      description: "Users with minimum session count since a date"
    },
    "order_summary" => {
      sql: "SELECT DATE(orders.created_at) AS order_date, COUNT(*) AS order_count, SUM(orders.total_amount) AS total_revenue, AVG(orders.total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM orders WHERE orders.status IN (:statuses) AND orders.created_at BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date GROUP BY DATE(orders.created_at) ORDER BY order_date DESC",
      params: [:start_date, :end_date, :statuses],
      description: "Order summary by date with status filter"
    }
  }.freeze

  MAX_ROW_LIMIT = 10_000
  QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30

  def index
    @available_reports = ALLOWED_REPORTS.transform_values { |v| v[:description] }
    @saved_reports = current_admin.saved_reports.order(created_at: :desc).limit(20)
  end

  def show
    report_key = params[:report_key]
    @report_config = ALLOWED_REPORTS[report_key]

    unless @report_config
      redirect_to admin_reports_path, alert: "Unknown report: #{report_key}"
      return
    end

    @report_key = report_key
    @required_params = @report_config[:params]
  end

  def execute
    report_key = params[:report_key]
    report_config = ALLOWED_REPORTS[report_key]

    unless report_config
      render json: { error: "Unknown report type" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end

    sanitized_params = sanitize_report_params(report_config, params[:report_params] || {})

    unless sanitized_params[:valid]
      render json: { error: sanitized_params[:errors].join(", ") }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end

    begin
      @results = execute_report(report_config[:sql], sanitized_params[:values])

      log_report_execution(report_key, sanitized_params[:values])

      respond_to do |format|
        format.html { render :results }
        format.json { render json: { columns: @results[:columns], rows: @results[:rows], row_count: @results[:row_count] } }
        format.csv { send_csv_report(report_key, @results) }
      end
    rescue ActiveRecord::QueryCanceled, ActiveRecord::StatementTimeout => e
      render json: { error: "Query timed out. Try narrowing your date range or filters." }, status: :request_timeout
    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
      Rails.logger.error("Report execution error: #{e.message}")
      render json: { error: "Report execution failed. Please contact support." }, status: :internal_server_error
    end
  end

  # Allow admins to create new report templates (reviewed before activation)
  def create_template
    template = ReportTemplate.new(
      name: params[:name],
      description: params[:description],
      sql_template: params[:sql_template],
      parameter_definitions: params[:parameter_definitions],
      created_by: current_admin,
      status: :pending_review # Must be reviewed by another admin before use
    )

    if template.save
      notify_admins_of_pending_template(template)
      redirect_to admin_reports_path, notice: "Report template submitted for review."
    else
      render :new_template, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  private

  def execute_report(sql_template, bind_params)
    connection = ActiveRecord::Base.connection

    # Use a read-only connection/role if available
    results = nil

    connection.transaction do
      connection.execute("SET LOCAL statement_timeout = '#{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s'") if connection.adapter_name == "PostgreSQL"

      # Use sanitize_sql_array for safe parameterized queries
      sanitized_sql = ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_array([sql_template, bind_params])

      # Wrap in read-only transaction to prevent any writes
      connection.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY") if connection.adapter_name == "PostgreSQL"

      raw_results = connection.exec_query(sanitized_sql)

      results = {
        columns: raw_results.columns,
        rows: raw_results.rows,
        row_count: raw_results.rows.length
      }

      # Rollback to ensure nothing is committed (extra safety for read-only)
      raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
    end

    results
  end

  def sanitize_report_params(report_config, raw_params)
    values = {}
    errors = []

    report_config[:params].each do |param_name|
      param_value = raw_params[param_name.to_s]

      if param_value.blank?
        errors << "#{param_name} is required"
        next
      end

      case param_name
      when :start_date, :end_date, :since_date
        begin
          parsed = Date.parse(param_value)
          if parsed < 10.years.ago.to_date || parsed > 1.day.from_now.to_date
            errors << "#{param_name} is out of acceptable range"
          else
            values[param_name] = parsed
          end
        rescue Date::Error
          errors << "#{param_name} must be a valid date (YYYY-MM-DD)"
        end
      when :row_limit
        limit = param_value.to_i
        if limit <= 0 || limit > MAX_ROW_LIMIT
          errors << "row_limit must be between 1 and #{MAX_ROW_LIMIT}"
        else
          values[param_name] = limit
        end
      when :min_sessions
        val = param_value.to_i
        if val < 0 || val > 100_000
          errors << "min_sessions must be between 0 and 100,000"
        else
          values[param_name] = val
        end
      when :statuses
        allowed_statuses = %w[pending confirmed shipped delivered cancelled refunded]
        requested = Array(param_value).map(&:to_s).select { |s| allowed_statuses.include?(s) }
        if requested.empty?
          errors << "At least one valid status is required (#{allowed_statuses.join(', ')})"
        else
          values[param_name] = requested
        end
      else
        # Reject unknown parameters
        errors << "Unknown parameter: #{param_name}"
      end
    end

    { valid: errors.empty?, values: values, errors: errors }
  end

  def send_csv_report(report_key, results)
    csv_data = CSV.generate do |csv|
      csv << results[:columns]
      results[:rows].each { |row| csv << row }
    end

    send_data csv_data,
              filename: "#{report_key}_#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.csv",
              type: "text/csv",
              disposition: "attachment"
  end

  def log_report_execution(report_key, params)
    AuditLog.create!(
      admin: current_admin,
      action: "report_executed",
      resource_type: "Report",
      resource_id: report_key,
      metadata: {
        report_key: report_key,
        parameters: params.transform_values(&:to_s),
        ip_address: request.remote_ip,
        user_agent: request.user_agent
      }
    )
  end

  def authenticate_admin!
    unless current_admin&.active?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Access denied"
    end
  end

  def authorize_reporting!
    unless current_admin&.has_permission?(:run_reports)
      redirect_to admin_root_path, alert: "You do not have reporting permissions"
    end
  end

  def notify_admins_of_pending_template(template)
    Admin.with_permission(:review_report_templates).find_each do |admin|
      AdminMailer.report_template_review_requested(admin, template).deliver_later
    end
  end
end
